description: Implement the CONSENSUS validation engine where multiple independent validator agents assess the same feature and a verdict is synthesized from their agreement or disagreement. Disagreements trigger deeper investigation via sequential analysis.
workflow_rationale: This is a feature workflow (not refactor, investigation, or migration). It introduces a brand-new validation engine (CONSENSUS) as a peer to the existing VALIDATE and FORGE engines defined in SPECIFICATION.md §3.1. The work adds new primitives (skills, agents, command, rule, template) that compose with existing primitives (parallel-validation, sequential-analysis, verdict-writer). No existing primitive is refactored or deprecated; old /validate flows continue working unchanged. Phases follow the ValidationForge primitive-dependency order: Rules → Skills → Agents → Command → Templates/Scripts → Integration/Dogfood, because higher-layer primitives reference lower-layer primitives by name.

phases.name: Rules, Specification, and Contract
phases.description: Codify the consensus engine contract before any skill/agent references it. Produces rules/consensus-engine.md (the authoritative contract for all downstream primitives) and updates SPECIFICATION.md §3.1 Engine 2 to reflect the implemented behavior. This phase must complete first because every skill/agent/command in later phases cites this rule file.

subtasks.description: Create rules/consensus-engine.md. This is the authoritative consensus contract. Required sections: (1) Engine Identity (role in the FORGE → VALIDATE → CONSENSUS triad, peer to rules/team-validation.md). (2) Roles table: Coordinator, Validator-N (≥2, default 3), Synthesizer. (3) File Ownership: each validator owns e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/ exclusively; synthesizer owns e2e-evidence/consensus/report.md; coordinator owns nothing. (4) Synthesis States table: UNANIMOUS_PASS → PASS (HIGH), UNANIMOUS_FAIL → FAIL (HIGH), MAJORITY_PASS → PASS (MEDIUM) after disagreement analysis, MAJORITY_FAIL → FAIL (MEDIUM) after disagreement analysis, SPLIT → DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED (LOW). (5) Confidence Formula: confidence = agreement_ratio where unanimous=HIGH, ≥⅔ after analysis=MEDIUM, <⅔=LOW. (6) Disagreement Protocol: when not unanimous, invoke sequential-analysis skill on the diverging criteria; if root cause found, re-resolve; if not, mark DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED and escalate. (7) Iron Rules carry-over (no mocks, no test files, evidence cited). (8) Relationship to /forge-plan --consensus: that is planning-time consensus (3 perspectives, 1 plan); this is execution-time consensus (N validators, 1 synthesized verdict) — distinct but philosophically aligned.

verification.expected: File exists and contains ≥12 references to the required contract terms

subtasks.notes: Created rules/consensus-engine.md with all 8 required sections: Engine Identity (positioned as peer to team-validation.md in the FORGE → VALIDATE → CONSENSUS triad), Roles table (Coordinator/Validator-N/Synthesizer with default 3 validators), File Ownership (validator-N/ exclusive, synthesizer owns report.md, coordinator owns nothing), Synthesis States table (UNANIMOUS_PASS/FAIL → HIGH, MAJORITY_PASS/FAIL → MEDIUM after analysis, SPLIT → DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED LOW), Confidence Formula (agreement_ratio with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW tiers), Disagreement Protocol (sequential-analysis over diverging criteria, 4 root-cause cases with distinct re-resolve strategies), Iron Rules carry-over from validation-discipline.md, Relationship to /forge-plan --consensus (distinguishes planning-time vs execution-time consensus). Verification grep returned 28 references to required contract terms (threshold ≥12). Committed as 2e4b54e.

subtasks.description: Update SPECIFICATION.md §3.1 Engine 2 (CONSENSUS) to replace the aspirational stub with the implemented contract. Keep the headline description ('Multi-reviewer agreement gate') but update Components list to reference: (a) agents/consensus-validator.md — independent validator agents (≥2, configurable via --validators N), (b) agents/consensus-synthesizer.md — synthesis agent, (c) skills/consensus-engine/SKILL.md — orchestration protocol, (d) skills/consensus-synthesis/SKILL.md — voting/confidence logic, (e) skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis/SKILL.md — sequential-analysis invocation for disagreements, (f) commands/validate-consensus.md — user entry point, (g) rules/consensus-engine.md — authoritative contract, (h) templates/consensus-report.md — unified report format. Cross-reference rules/consensus-engine.md as the source of truth.

verification.expected: At least 4 lines referencing new consensus primitives in SPECIFICATION.md §3.1

subtasks.notes: Updated SPECIFICATION.md §3.1 Engine 2 (CONSENSUS). Kept 'Multi-reviewer agreement gate' headline; expanded description to reference independent validator agents, synthesis agent, and sequential-analysis for disagreements. Replaced the 4-item stub Components list with an 8-item list citing: (a) agents/consensus-validator.md, (b) agents/consensus-synthesizer.md, (c) skills/consensus-engine/SKILL.md, (d) skills/consensus-synthesis/SKILL.md, (e) skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis/SKILL.md, (f) commands/validate-consensus.md, (g) rules/consensus-engine.md, (h) templates/consensus-report.md. Added an authoritative-contract callout pointing to rules/consensus-engine.md as the single source of truth. Verification: grep returned 6 hits for the new primitives (≥4 required). Committed as 9cc3140.

phases.name: Consensus Skills (Protocol Layer)
phases.description: Create the three skills that encode the consensus protocol. Skills are the Layer 2 protocol primitives that agents and commands reference by name. Must follow phase-1 so the skills can cite rules/consensus-engine.md as their contract.

subtasks.description: Create skills/consensus-engine/SKILL.md — the top-level consensus orchestration skill. Frontmatter: name: consensus-engine, description: 'Orchestrate N independent validator agents assessing the same feature; synthesize verdicts into a single consensus report with confidence scoring.', triggers: ['consensus validation', 'multi-reviewer validation', 'validate with consensus', 'unanimous validation', 'consensus verdict']. Body: (1) When to Use (high-stakes features, regression review, pre-ship gate). (2) Protocol: Step 1 read validation plan; Step 2 spawn ≥2 validators via Task tool with run_in_background=true, each receiving the full journey list and its own evidence subdir e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/; Step 3 monitor validators (never interfere); Step 4 when all complete, spawn consensus-synthesizer agent; Step 5 if disagreement detected, invoke skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis; Step 6 emit unified consensus report via templates/consensus-report.md. (3) File Ownership table (cite rules/consensus-engine.md). (4) Integration: pairs with parallel-validation (reuses parallel orchestration patterns), sequential-analysis (for disagreement root cause), verdict-writer (synthesizer is a specialization), preflight (run before spawning).

verification.command: test -f skills/consensus-engine/SKILL.md && head -8 skills/consensus-engine/SKILL.md | grep -E 'name:|description:|triggers:' | wc -l | tr -d ' '

subtasks.notes: Created skills/consensus-engine/SKILL.md — the top-level consensus orchestration skill. Frontmatter includes name: consensus-engine, description referencing N independent validators synthesized with confidence scoring, and all 5 required triggers ('consensus validation', 'multi-reviewer validation', 'validate with consensus', 'unanimous validation', 'consensus verdict'). Body covers: (1) When to Use with high-stakes/regression/pre-ship/flake-hunt/audit scenarios plus explicit do-not-use cases; (2) 6-step Protocol (read plan → spawn ≥2 validators via Task with run_in_background=true each getting identical journey list + exclusive e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-N/ subdir → monitor without interference → spawn synthesizer only after all complete → invoke consensus-disagreement-analysis on non-unanimous journeys → emit unified report via templates/consensus-report.md); (3) File Ownership table citing rules/consensus-engine.md with coordinator-owns-nothing invariant; (4) Integration table linking parallel-validation, sequential-analysis, verdict-writer, preflight, consensus-synthesis, consensus-disagreement-analysis, and templates/consensus-report.md; (5) Iron Rules carry-over. Verification command returned 3 (expected 3). Committed as 4164ddc.

subtasks.description: Create skills/consensus-synthesis/SKILL.md — the voting and confidence-scoring skill used by the consensus-synthesizer agent. Frontmatter: name: consensus-synthesis, description: 'Synthesize N per-validator verdicts into a single consensus verdict with confidence scoring based on agreement level.', triggers: ['synthesize consensus', 'consensus verdict', 'validator agreement', 'confidence score']. Body: (1) Input: N validator reports at e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/report.md. (2) Vote Tabulation: per-criterion PASS/FAIL counts across validators. (3) Synthesis Rules: UNANIMOUS → use that verdict with HIGH confidence; MAJORITY (≥⅔) → use majority with MEDIUM confidence (after disagreement analysis confirms minority was mistaken); SPLIT (<⅔) → DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED with LOW confidence. (4) Confidence Formula: c = agreement_ratio; agreement_ratio = majority_count / total_validators; HIGH if 1.0, MEDIUM if 0.67 ≤ x < 1.0, LOW if < 0.67. (5) Anti-patterns: never silently drop a minority verdict; always record dissent in the report; never use 'inconclusive' — use DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED. (6) Output schema matching templates/consensus-report.md.

verification.expected: At least 7 (all synthesis states and confidence levels present)

subtasks.notes: Created skills/consensus-synthesis/SKILL.md — the voting and confidence-scoring skill used by the consensus-synthesizer agent. Frontmatter includes name: consensus-synthesis, description referencing N per-validator verdicts synthesized with confidence scoring, and all 4 required triggers ('synthesize consensus', 'consensus verdict', 'validator agreement', 'confidence score'). Body covers: (1) Input section describing N validator reports at e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/report.md with preconditions (all N present, non-empty, citing evidence); (2) Vote Tabulation with both per-journey and per-criterion matrices; (3) Synthesis Rules table mapping UNANIMOUS_PASS/FAIL → HIGH, MAJORITY_PASS/FAIL (≥⅔) → MEDIUM after disagreement analysis, SPLIT (<⅔) → DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED LOW; (4) Confidence Formula with agreement_ratio = majority_count / total_validators formula plus worked examples for 3-validator and 5-validator configurations; (5) Anti-patterns table (8 items: silently dropping minorities, skipping disagreement analysis, using 'inconclusive', averaging confidence, upgrading for evidence quality, premature report emission, writing to validator-N/, fabricating tie-breakers); (6) Output Schema matching templates/consensus-report.md with per-journey sections (state, verdict, confidence, agreement_ratio, validator list, vote tabulation, per-criterion tabulation, dissenting opinions, disagreement analysis, reasoning) plus overall run verdict block and stdout summary; (7) Integration table cross-referencing rules/consensus-engine.md, consensus-engine skill, consensus-disagreement-analysis, consensus-synthesizer agent, verdict-writer, consensus-report template; (8) Iron Rules carry-over (no silent dissent, no partial synthesis, no writing to validator-N/, no 'inconclusive', no confidence inflation, no single-validator synthesis, cited evidence required). Verification grep returned 44 hits for synthesis states and confidence terms (threshold ≥7). Committed as e6844f4.

subtasks.description: Create skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis/SKILL.md — the disagreement resolution skill that wraps sequential-analysis. Frontmatter: name: consensus-disagreement-analysis, description: 'When validators disagree on a consensus verdict, run root cause analysis to determine whose evidence is stronger.', triggers: ['validators disagree', 'consensus disagreement', 'resolve disagreement', 'split verdict']. Body: (1) Triggered when consensus-synthesis detects non-unanimous verdict. (2) Protocol: Step 1 identify the diverging criterion (which criterion did validators rate differently?); Step 2 for each diverging criterion, load BOTH validators' evidence files; Step 3 invoke skills/sequential-analysis with the FAIL verdict as symptom and the PASS verdict's evidence as a hypothesis to test; Step 4 apply sequential-analysis Phase 1–4 (Symptom → Hypothesize → Investigate → Conclude); Step 5 emit a resolution: MINORITY_CORRECT (flip majority), MAJORITY_CORRECT (keep majority, record dissent), UNRESOLVABLE (keep as DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED). (3) Evidence path: e2e-evidence/consensus/disagreement-analysis/step-{NN}-*.md. (4) Integration: strictly read-only against validator subdirectories — NEVER modify another validator's evidence.

verification.expected: At least 5 (all resolution states and the sequential-analysis reference)

subtasks.notes: Created skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis/SKILL.md with full frontmatter (name, description, 4 triggers), 5-step protocol wrapping skills/sequential-analysis, evidence path under e2e-evidence/consensus/disagreement-analysis/, three resolution states (MINORITY_CORRECT / MAJORITY_CORRECT / UNRESOLVABLE), strict read-only discipline against validator-N/ directories, and integration/iron-rules sections consistent with rules/consensus-engine.md and skills/consensus-synthesis. Verification grep returned 31 matches (required ≥5).

phases.name: Consensus Agents
phases.description: Create the two agents that implement the consensus-validator role (independent assessor) and the consensus-synthesizer role (cross-validator verdict writer). Must follow phase-2 because agents reference the skills by name.

subtasks.description: Create agents/consensus-validator.md — the independent validator agent template. Frontmatter: description: 'Independent validator that assesses a feature against PASS criteria with its own evidence, unaware of other validators verdicts.', capabilities: ['independent-assessment', 'evidence-capture', 'per-validator-verdict', 'real-system-interaction']. Body: (1) Identity: Role=independent assessor; Input=validation plan + assigned validator index N; Output=e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/report.md; Constraint=NEVER read another validator's evidence subdirectory, NEVER coordinate with peer validators, capture evidence independently. (2) Protocol: Step 1 receive journeys and validator index N from coordinator; Step 2 run full 7-phase pipeline (preflight → execute → evidence capture → per-journey verdict) writing ONLY to e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/; Step 3 produce per-journey PASS/FAIL verdicts following agents/verdict-writer.md structure; Step 4 write evidence-inventory.txt; Step 5 signal completion to coordinator. (3) Independence Rules: never use shared test fixtures across validators, each validator uses a fresh system state where possible (fresh browser profile, fresh simulator, fresh curl session), vary your approach slightly (e.g., different viewport, different auth user) to surface platform-dependent bugs. (4) Iron Rules (no mocks, real evidence, cite proof).

verification.command: test -f agents/consensus-validator.md && grep -c -E 'validator-\{N\}|validator-N|Independent|independence|NEVER read another|capabilities:' agents/consensus-validator.md

subtasks.notes: Created agents/consensus-validator.md with required frontmatter (description matches spec, capabilities = independent-assessment, evidence-capture, per-validator-verdict, real-system-interaction). Body covers all four required sections: (1) Identity with role=independent assessor, input=plan + validator index N, output=e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/report.md, and the explicit NEVER read peer subdirectory / NEVER coordinate / capture independently constraints. (2) Protocol with all 5 steps (receive assignment+index, run 7-phase pipeline writing only to validator-{N}/, per-journey verdicts following verdict-writer.md structure, evidence-inventory.txt generation, signal completion). (3) Independence Rules including no shared fixtures, fresh state per platform (browser profile/simulator/curl session/working dir), and approach variation (viewport/user/data/timing) to surface platform-dependent bugs. (4) Iron Rules carry-over (no mocks, real evidence, cite proof, no peer references). Added Anti-Patterns table and Handoff section reinforcing independence. Verification grep returned 21 matches (threshold ≥5).

subtasks.description: Create agents/consensus-synthesizer.md — the cross-validator verdict synthesizer. Frontmatter: description: 'Reads all validator reports, applies consensus-synthesis voting rules, handles disagreements via consensus-disagreement-analysis, produces the unified consensus verdict.', capabilities: ['cross-validator-synthesis', 'vote-tabulation', 'disagreement-resolution', 'unified-report']. Body: (1) Identity: Role=synthesizer; Input=N validator reports at e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/report.md; Output=e2e-evidence/consensus/report.md; Constraint=read every validator report and every cited evidence file (do NOT trust verdicts on their face — re-read the cited evidence). (2) Protocol: Step 1 enumerate validator reports; Step 2 per-criterion tabulate PASS/FAIL votes across validators; Step 3 apply skills/consensus-synthesis rules (UNANIMOUS/MAJORITY/SPLIT); Step 4 for non-unanimous, invoke skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis; Step 5 assemble the final report using templates/consensus-report.md; Step 6 emit confidence score and overall verdict. (3) Disagreement Recording: even when the majority wins, always record the dissenting validator's evidence and reasoning in a 'Dissenting Opinions' section. (4) Anti-patterns: never average confidence levels, never mark a split verdict PASS without resolution, never discard a minority dissent.

verification.expected: At least 5 (the required cross-references and key mechanics)

phases.name: Command Entry Point and Report Template
phases.description: Create the user-facing /validate-consensus command and the consensus-report template. Can run in parallel with phase-5 (scripts) because they touch different file sets. Must follow phase-3 (agents) so the command can reference the agents by name.

subtasks.description: Create commands/validate-consensus.md — the user entry point for consensus validation. Frontmatter: name: validate-consensus, description: 'Spawn N independent validators to assess the same feature and synthesize a unified consensus verdict with confidence scoring.', triggers: ['validate consensus', 'consensus validation', 'multi-reviewer validate', 'unanimous validate']. Body mirrors commands/validate-team.md structure: (1) Usage: /validate-consensus [--validators N] [--scope PATH] [--platform X] [--fix]; default N=3. (2) Flags table: --validators (2-5, default 3), --scope (subset to validate), --platform (override auto-detect), --fix (on disagreement, apply sweep-controller fix loop), --strict (require unanimous PASS to ship, fail on DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED). (3) Architecture ASCII diagram: Coordinator → N validators → Synthesizer → unified report. (4) Workflow: Invoke skills/consensus-engine for orchestration. (5) Agent Roles table (coordinator=you, consensus-validator × N, consensus-synthesizer). (6) Evidence Structure under e2e-evidence/consensus/. (7) Verdict Semantics: PASS HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW or FAIL HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW or DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED. (8) Integration: Pairs with /validate (single-validator mode), /validate-team (multi-platform mode), /validate-sweep (autonomous fix loop). (9) Pre-Pipeline Read Config section mirroring commands/validate.md for ~/.claude/.vf-config.json consumption.

verification.expected: At least 8 (command name, flags, agent refs, skill ref, confidence levels, disagreement state)

subtasks.notes: Created commands/validate-consensus.md mirroring commands/validate-team.md structure with consensus-specific semantics. Frontmatter includes name: validate-consensus, description referencing N independent validators + unified consensus verdict with confidence scoring, and all 4 required triggers ('validate consensus', 'consensus validation', 'multi-reviewer validate', 'unanimous validate'). Body covers all 9 required sections: (1) Usage block with --validators, --scope, --platform, --fix, --strict flags; (2) Flags table documenting --validators (2-5, default 3), --scope subset, --platform override, --fix sweep-controller delegation, --strict unanimous-PASS-to-ship gate; (3) ASCII architecture diagram showing Coordinator → preflight → create-validation-plan → skills/consensus-engine → N parallel consensus-validator Tasks with run_in_background=true → consensus-synthesizer → unified report; (4) Workflow section delegating orchestration to skills/consensus-engine with its 6-step protocol summarized; (5) Agent Roles table (coordinator=you, consensus-validator × N with exclusive validator-{N}/ write, consensus-synthesizer with exclusive report.md write) citing rules/consensus-engine.md; (6) Evidence Structure tree showing e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/ per-validator dirs, disagreement-analysis/ synthesizer-owned subdir, and report.md; (7) Verdict Semantics including synthesis state table (UNANIMOUS_PASS/FAIL → HIGH, MAJORITY_PASS/FAIL after analysis → MEDIUM, SPLIT → DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED LOW), confidence formula with agreement_ratio, weakest-link overall rule, and --strict behavior; (8) Integration table pairing with /validate, /validate-team, /validate-sweep, /validate-fix, /validate-ci, skills/consensus-engine, skills/consensus-synthesis, skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis, rules/consensus-engine.md, templates/consensus-report.md; (9) Pre-Pipeline Read Config block mirroring commands/validate.md including ~/.claude/.vf-config.json consumption, enforcement/evidence_dir/platform defaults, --validators [2,5] clamp, VF_VERBOSE summary, and a strict/standard/permissive enforcement table adapted to consensus (preflight required, evidence citations mandatory, DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED handling, mock blocking, max fix attempts, minimum validators). Added Iron Rules carry-over block, Examples block with 5 invocation patterns, and Output/Exit-behavior block with one-line stdout summary matching the consensus-synthesizer spec. Verification grep returned 43 matches for required terms (threshold ≥8). Committed as f55b186.

subtasks.description: Create templates/consensus-report.md — the unified consensus report template. Sections: (1) Header metadata (Feature, Platform, Validators count, Synthesis date, Overall Verdict, Confidence). (2) Summary table per criterion: Criterion | V1 | V2 | V3 | Consensus | Confidence — showing each validator's individual verdict and the synthesized outcome. (3) Validator Reports Links: per-validator report path, evidence-inventory path, verdict summary. (4) Agreement Analysis: agreement_ratio per criterion, overall agreement_ratio. (5) Dissenting Opinions: for each non-unanimous criterion, the minority validator's cited evidence and reasoning. (6) Disagreement Resolution: for each non-unanimous criterion, the sequential-analysis outcome (MINORITY_CORRECT / MAJORITY_CORRECT / UNRESOLVABLE) and the root cause. (7) Final Verdict block with explicit formula: overall_verdict = majority_verdict IF majority_confidence ≥ MEDIUM ELSE DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED. (8) Evidence Inventory: full list of files across all validator subdirectories with byte counts. Use {{PLACEHOLDER}} convention from templates/verdict.md and templates/e2e-report.md.

verification.expected: At least 8 (multiple placeholder tokens and all required section keywords)

subtasks.notes: Created templates/consensus-report.md with all 8 required sections following the {{PLACEHOLDER}} convention from templates/verdict.md and templates/e2e-report.md. Sections: (1) Header metadata with Feature, Platform, Validators count, Synthesis Date, Overall Verdict, Confidence, plus synthesizer attribution and rule cross-references. (2) Per-Criterion Consensus Matrix (Journey | Criterion | V1 | V2 | V3 | Consensus | Confidence) showing each validator's individual verdict and the synthesized outcome, with Pass Rate summary. (3) Validator Reports table with per-validator report.md path, evidence-inventory.txt path, and verdict summary for each of the 3 default validators. (4) Agreement Analysis with per-criterion pass_count/fail_count/agreement_ratio/State table, overall_agreement_ratio block including computation method, journey-state counts (UNANIMOUS_PASS/UNANIMOUS_FAIL/MAJORITY_PASS/MAJORITY_FAIL/SPLIT), and weakest-link journey. (5) Dissenting Opinions section preserving minority validator's cited evidence and verbatim reasoning per non-unanimous criterion, with explicit None (UNANIMOUS run) fallback guidance. (6) Disagreement Resolution section documenting sequential-analysis outcome (MINORITY_CORRECT / MAJORITY_CORRECT / UNRESOLVABLE), diverging criterion, sequential-analysis evidence path, root cause, and final action taken per non-unanimous criterion. (7) Final Verdict block with the explicit formula overall_verdict = majority_verdict IF majority_confidence >= MEDIUM ELSE DISAGREEMENT_UNRESOLVED plus applied majority_verdict/majority_confidence/overall_verdict/overall_confidence lines and reasoning. (8) Evidence Inventory listing files across validator-1/, validator-2/, validator-3/, and disagreement-analysis/ subdirectories with byte counts, total evidence file/byte counts, and zero-byte file detection per Iron Rule #8. Template also includes a stdout summary block matching the one-line format from skills/consensus-synthesis/SKILL.md. Verification: grep -c -E '\{\{[A-Z_]+\}\}|Consensus|Dissenting|Disagreement|Agreement' templates/consensus-report.md returned 29 (threshold ≥8). Committed as 48f6df3.

phases.name: Evidence Aggregation Helper Script
phases.description: Create scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh — a Bash helper that inventories all validator evidence subdirectories and emits a machine-readable summary the synthesizer consumes. Independent of phase-4 (commands/templates) but depends on phase-1 (the file ownership layout from rules/consensus-engine.md).

subtasks.description: Create scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh. Follow the pattern of scripts/evidence-collector.sh and scripts/verify-setup.sh: set -euo pipefail, helper functions, clear [OK]/[MISSING] output. Behavior: (1) Accept EVIDENCE_DIR=${EVIDENCE_DIR:-e2e-evidence/consensus} as env var. (2) Enumerate validator-* subdirectories; verify each has a report.md and evidence-inventory.txt (error if fewer than 2 validators). (3) For each validator: count evidence files, parse PASS/FAIL verdicts per journey from report.md (grep for 'Verdict: PASS' / 'Verdict: FAIL' lines). (4) Emit a consolidated JSON-ish summary to stdout: validator_count, evidence_totals, per-journey vote counts. (5) Exit 0 if ≥2 validators with valid reports; exit 1 otherwise. (6) No network calls, no file modifications — strictly read-only aggregation.

verification.command: bash -n scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh && test -x scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh && head -3 scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh | grep -c '#!/.*bash'
verification.expected: 1 (script has bash shebang, is syntactically valid, is executable)

subtasks.notes:
Created scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh following the patterns of scripts/evidence-collector.sh and scripts/verify-setup.sh (set -euo pipefail, ok()/missing() helpers, [OK]/[MISSING] output routed to stderr). Behavior implemented: (1) EVIDENCE_DIR env var with default e2e-evidence/consensus; (2) enumerates validator-* subdirectories under EVIDENCE_DIR with find -maxdepth 1 -type d, verifies each has both report.md and evidence-inventory.txt (exit 1 if fewer than 2 validators pass the check, with emit_empty_summary helper producing JSON with error code); (3) per validator: counts evidence files (find -type f excluding report.md and evidence-inventory.txt), counts overall PASS/FAIL verdicts via grep -cE 'Verdict:[*[:space:]]*(PASS|FAIL)' (regex tolerates both plain 'Verdict: PASS' and markdown '**Verdict:** PASS'), parses per-journey verdicts via awk state machine that pairs '## Journey: NAME' headers with the first subsequent Verdict line; (4) emits consolidated JSON-ish summary on stdout with evidence_dir, validator_count, invalid_validators, evidence_totals.files, per-validator {name, evidence_files, pass, fail}, and per-journey {journey, pass, fail} vote counts; (5) exit 0 iff valid_count >= 2 else exit 1; (6) strictly read-only — uses an in-memory JOURNEY_TALLY shell variable instead of a tempfile to avoid any filesystem writes inside EVIDENCE_DIR or /tmp.

Verification:
- Plan verification: `bash -n scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh && test -x scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh && head -3 scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh | grep -c '#!/.*bash'` returned 1 (expected 1).
- Smoke test (missing dir): `EVIDENCE_DIR=/tmp/claude/nonexistent-X bash scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh` exited 1 with clear [MISSING] stderr and evidence_dir_missing JSON.
- Functional test with 3 validators (V1 PASS+PASS, V2 PASS+FAIL, V3 missing inventory): exit 0, valid validator_count=2, invalid_validators=1, per-journey tally correctly shows login {pass:2, fail:0} and checkout {pass:1, fail:1} (surfaces disagreement).
- Single-validator test: exit 1. No-validators test: exit 1.
- Read-only test: find -exec stat before/after showed EVIDENCE_DIR unchanged after execution.

phases.name: Documentation and Index Updates

phases.name: Integration Benchmarks and Dogfood Verification
phases.description: End-to-end verification that the consensus engine is wired correctly. Run existing benchmark scripts to verify structural integrity of new skills/commands, cross-check all references resolve, and perform a dry-run dogfood of /validate-consensus against the ValidationForge repo itself (a sample journey, e.g., 'README.md existence and key section presence') to produce a real consensus report.

subtasks.description: Run structural benchmark scripts to verify new skills and command pass existing quality gates: bash scripts/benchmark/validate-skills.sh (verifies all 44 SKILL.md files have valid frontmatter — the 3 new consensus skills must pass); bash scripts/benchmark/validate-cmds.sh (verifies all 16 commands have valid frontmatter — /validate-consensus must pass); bash scripts/benchmark/test-hooks.sh (regression check — must still pass, no hooks were modified).

verification.command: bash scripts/benchmark/validate-skills.sh && bash scripts/benchmark/validate-cmds.sh && bash scripts/benchmark/test-hooks.sh

subtasks.notes: All three structural benchmark scripts pass with exit 0. validate-skills.sh: 44/44 pass (including 3 new consensus skills: consensus-engine, consensus-synthesis, consensus-disagreement-analysis). validate-cmds.sh: 16/16 pass (including validate-consensus). test-hooks.sh: 18/18 pass (regression confirmed, no hooks modified). Evidence captured at e2e-evidence/subtask-7-1-benchmarks/.

subtasks.description: Cross-reference linting: verify every primitive referenced in the new files actually exists on disk. Commands must reference existing skills/agents; skills must reference existing skills/templates; agents must reference existing skills. Run a grep-based link check: for each mention of 'skills/<name>' or 'agents/<name>' or 'templates/<name>' in the new files, verify the target file exists. No broken cross-references permitted.

verification.instructions: Walk through skills/consensus-engine/SKILL.md, skills/consensus-synthesis/SKILL.md, skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis/SKILL.md, agents/consensus-validator.md, agents/consensus-synthesizer.md, commands/validate-consensus.md, rules/consensus-engine.md, templates/consensus-report.md. For each 'skills/X', 'agents/Y', 'templates/Z', 'commands/W', 'rules/R' mention, confirm the target file exists on disk. Document findings in subtask notes. Zero broken references required.

subtasks.description: Dogfood dry-run: execute /validate-consensus in ORCHESTRATION mode against a trivial journey on the ValidationForge repo itself. Example journey: 'README.md exists, contains Iron Rule, contains Installation section, contains Verification Status section'. Spawn 2 lightweight consensus-validator agents (e.g., via the Task tool with run_in_background=true, each reading README.md and producing a report at e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/report.md), then invoke the consensus-synthesizer. Verify the final e2e-evidence/consensus/report.md is produced per templates/consensus-report.md, contains a confidence score, and represents all 6 acceptance criteria (AC1 ≥2 validators spawned, AC2 separate evidence subdirs, AC3 synthesis handled unanimous-PASS or whatever state occurred, AC4 if disagreement occurred, analysis was invoked, AC5 confidence present, AC6 evidence-inventory.txt per validator).

steps: Ensure e2e-evidence/consensus/ does not already contain a conflicting run
Spawn 2 consensus-validator agents with run_in_background=true, each assigned the README.md-sections journey
Wait for both validators to complete (poll TaskOutput)
Verify e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-1/report.md and e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-2/report.md exist and are non-empty
Verify the report contains: Overall Verdict, Confidence (HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW), per-criterion vote table, Evidence Inventory
Confirm all 6 acceptance criteria from spec.md are observably satisfied in the generated artifacts

subtasks.notes: Dogfood dry-run complete. Spawned 2 consensus-validator agents (run_in_background=true) targeting README.md sections journey; both returned PASS with HIGH confidence and independent evidence captured via different command strategies (V1: stat + grep -n; V2: test -f + awk + sed). Each validator wrote to its own e2e-evidence/consensus/validator-{N}/ subdirectory with non-empty report.md, evidence-inventory.txt, preflight.txt, and 4 per-criterion evidence files. Synthesizer produced e2e-evidence/consensus/report.md (16684 bytes, 218 lines) matching templates/consensus-report.md structure: Overall Verdict=PASS, Confidence=HIGH, agreement_ratio=1.00, UNANIMOUS_PASS on all 4 criteria. Zero-byte evidence count: 0. Report includes explicit "Acceptance-Criteria Coverage" table documenting satisfaction of AC1-AC6.

subtasks.description: Write a short verification summary at e2e-evidence/consensus/verification-summary.md documenting which acceptance criteria are satisfied by which generated artifact. Include: table of 6 acceptance criteria → evidence file + observed content. This is the ship-readiness gate for the spec.

verification.instructions: Read e2e-evidence/consensus/verification-summary.md. Confirm: (1) All 6 acceptance criteria from .auto-claude/specs/019-consensus-engine/spec.md are listed; (2) Each criterion cites a specific file path from the phase-7-3 dogfood run; (3) Each criterion has an observation sentence describing what was observed in that file; (4) An overall ship/no-ship recommendation with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW confidence.

parallel_groups.reason: phase-4 writes only to commands/ and templates/; phase-5 writes only to scripts/. They share no files, both depend on phase-1 (phase-5) or phase-3 (phase-4) which will already be complete at the point both are eligible.

phase-3 internally has two subtasks (consensus-validator and consensus-synthesizer) that write to different agent files and can be implemented in parallel by one coder; the phase as a whole is a single block.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: 1.2x — most phases are strictly sequential because later primitives reference earlier ones. Only phase-4 and phase-5 can run truly in parallel. A single worker is recommended because the coder must maintain cross-reference consistency; parallel workers risk divergent vocabulary across skills/agents/commands.

acceptance_criteria: At least 2 independent validator agents assess the same feature
Each validator captures its own evidence independently
Verdict synthesis algorithm handles agreement, partial agreement, and disagreement
Disagreements trigger root cause investigation with sequential analysis
CONSENSUS verdict includes confidence score based on agent agreement level
Evidence from all validators preserved in separate subdirectories

verification_steps.expected_outcome: All 7 existing hooks still pass (no regressions from doc/index edits)

verification_steps.name: Aggregation script smoke test
verification_steps.command: bash -n scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh && EVIDENCE_DIR=/tmp/nonexistent bash scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh; echo exit=$?
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Script is syntactically valid; exits non-zero (with clear error) when evidence dir is missing

verification_steps.name: Cross-reference link check
verification_steps.command: for f in skills/consensus-engine/SKILL.md skills/consensus-synthesis/SKILL.md skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis/SKILL.md agents/consensus-validator.md agents/consensus-synthesizer.md commands/validate-consensus.md rules/consensus-engine.md templates/consensus-report.md; do test -f "$f" && echo "[OK] $f" || echo "[MISSING] $f"; done
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All 8 new primitives exist on disk

verification_steps.name: Acceptance-criteria manual mapping
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All 6 acceptance criteria listed with specific evidence file citations from the dogfood run

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk. The change introduces a new engine with new primitives (rules, skills, agents, command, template, script) that compose with existing primitives and appear in user-facing docs. Risks: (a) inconsistent vocabulary between files → caught by cross-reference linting; (b) broken frontmatter → caught by benchmark scripts; (c) acceptance criteria not actually satisfied by the markdown → caught by dogfood run + verification-summary gate. Iron Rule forbids unit tests, so verification is benchmark-driven plus a dogfood e2e run that produces real evidence, which is the ValidationForge methodology being applied to itself.

unit_tests.reasoning: ValidationForge Iron Rule forbids unit tests and mocks. Verification uses structural benchmarks + dogfood e2e, not unit tests.

commands: # Dogfood: spawn 2 consensus-validator agents + consensus-synthesizer against README.md-sections journey
flows: each validator produces independent evidence in its own subdir
synthesizer reads all validator reports and produces unified consensus report with confidence score
if split verdict occurs, disagreement analysis is invoked and recorded

checks: rules/consensus-engine.md contains the full contract (roles, ownership, synthesis states, confidence formula, disagreement protocol)
SPECIFICATION.md §3.1 Engine 2 references the new primitives by path
skills/consensus-engine/SKILL.md, skills/consensus-synthesis/SKILL.md, skills/consensus-disagreement-analysis/SKILL.md all have valid frontmatter
agents/consensus-validator.md and agents/consensus-synthesizer.md have valid frontmatter and identity/protocol/rules structure
commands/validate-consensus.md has --validators flag defaulting to 3 and mirrors /validate-team structure
templates/consensus-report.md has placeholders for Overall Verdict, Confidence, per-criterion vote table, Dissenting Opinions, Disagreement Resolution, Evidence Inventory
scripts/consensus-aggregate.sh is executable, idempotent, read-only, follows existing scripts conventions
README.md, CLAUDE.md, COMMANDS.md, SKILLS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md all reference the new primitives and counts are consistent across all 5 files
Dogfood run in phase-7-3 produces the expected e2e-evidence/consensus/ artifact tree
e2e-evidence/consensus/verification-summary.md maps each of the 6 acceptance criteria to a specific observed artifact
